author: isanghao
title: JS API E2E test cleanup fails with Permission denied on Electron DLL on Windows
signature: rm cannot remove electron dist dll Permission denied|Flaky Test

failed_jobs.name: JS API (26)
failed_jobs.symptom: E2E after-all hook fails: rm -rf demo-electron-app-project Permission denied on d3dcompiler_47.dll

failed_jobs.name: ci/gha_overall_status_windows
failed_jobs.symptom: Overall status check fails because JS API (26) failed

key_errors:
exec error: Error: Command failed: rm -rf demo-electron-app-project
rm: cannot remove 'demo-electron-app-project/node_modules/electron/dist/d3dcompiler_47.dll': Permission denied

1) E2E testing for OpenVINO as an Electron dependency.
     "after all" hook for "should run electron package and verify output":
   Error: Command failed: rm -rf demo-electron-app-project
rm: cannot remove 'demo-electron-app-project/node_modules/electron/dist/d3dcompiler_47.dll': Permission denied
root_cause: The E2E Mocha test 'after all' cleanup hook calls 'rm -rf demo-electron-app-project' (Unix-style shell delete via Git Bash on Windows). Both actual tests passed (install deps + run electron). The cleanup fails because d3dcompiler_47.dll inside node_modules/electron/dist/ has its read-only/locked attribute set on Windows — either because Electron holds a file handle on the DLL after the electron process exits, or because npm sets read-only attributes on installed binaries. The Unix 'rm -rf' command on Windows Git Bash does not strip read-only attributes before deletion, causing Permission denied.
reproduction_steps:
1. Run 'npm run test:e2e' in src/bindings/js/node on a Windows host.
2. After the electron test completes, observe whether 'rm -rf demo-electron-app-project' fails on d3dcompiler_47.dll.
recommended_actions: Replace 'rm -rf demo-electron-app-project' in the E2E test after-all hook with a cross-platform delete using 'rimraf' (already available as a dev dependency in the Node.js ecosystem) or PowerShell 'Remove-Item -Force -Recurse demo-electron-app-project'.
Alternatively, add 'icacls demo-electron-app-project /grant Everyone:F /T' before the rm call on Windows to strip read-only attributes.
Add error handling to the after-all hook so cleanup failures do not fail the test suite (using try/catch or Mocha's 'this.timeout' with error suppression).
prevention_strategies: Use rimraf or a Node.js cross-platform delete (fs.rmSync with {recursive: true, force: true} in Node >= 14) instead of 'rm -rf' in test cleanup hooks to handle Windows file permission/locking edge cases.
historical_context: First occurrence of this specific failure signature. Related to Windows file permission issues with Electron DLL files during test cleanup.
